sections.title: 1. Concept Overview
sections.body:
According to the CBSE Syllabus 2023-24, this chapter has been renumbered as Chapter 6.
Introduction
The human body is a complex machine performing tons of functions and processes to maintain and sustain life. Explore how the body controls its movements and coordinates its actions with other parts of the body and the environment by exploring notes for Class 10 Chapter 7 Control and Coordination.
The Nervous System
Movement in Organisms
The ability of organisms to move certain body parts is movement.
When they move from one place to another, it is called locomotion.
Organisms show movements in response to stimuli.
Introduction to Control & Coordination
- Organisms move in response to various kinds of stimuli like light, heat, nutrients/food, etc.
- All the activities in animals are controlled and coordinated by the nervous and endocrine systems.
- Hormones are chemical messengers which assist the nervous system in carrying out various functions. They are secreted by endocrine glands.
- Hormones in plants coordinate the movements.
For more information on Nervous System, watch the below video
To know more about The Nervous System, visit here.
The Nervous System
Neuron
Neuron is the str…

- [CBSE | Central Board of Secondary Education](https://cbseacademic.nic.in/?ref=w3use)

sections.title: 2. Key Definitions
sections.body:
**Definitions extracted from crawled pages:**

- The ability of organisms to move certain body parts is movement.
- When they move from one place to another, it is called locomotion.
- All the activities in animals are controlled and coordinated by the nervous and endocrine systems.
- Hormones are chemical messengers which assist the nervous system in carrying out various functions. They are secreted by endocrine glands.
- Neuron is the structural and functional unit of the nervous system.
- The impulse transmission is faster in myelinated neurons.
- The central nervous system (CNS) is made up of the brain and the spinal cord. The functions of different parts of the brain are:
- The cerebrum is responsible for reasoning, logic, emotions, speech, memory, visual processing, recognition of auditory and taste stimuli, etc.

sections.title: 3. Core Concepts
sections.body:
According to the CBSE Syllabus 2023-24, this chapter has been renumbered as Chapter 6.
Introduction
The human body is a complex machine performing tons of functions and processes to maintain and sustain life. Explore how the body controls its movements and coordinates its actions with other parts of the body and the environment by exploring notes for Class 10 Chapter 7 Control and Coordination.
The Nervous System
Movement in Organisms
The ability of organisms to move certain body parts is movement.
When they move from one place to another, it is called locomotion.
Organisms show movements in response to stimuli.
Introduction to Control & Coordination
- Organisms move in response to various kinds of stimuli like light, heat, nutrients/food, etc.
- All the activities in animals are controlled and coordinated by the nervous and endocrine systems.
- Hormones are chemical messengers which assist the nervous system in carrying out various functions. They are secreted by endocrine glands.
- Hormones in plants coordinate the movements.
For more information on Nervous System, watch the below video
To know more about The Nervous System, visit here.
The Nervous System
Neuron
Neuron is the structural and functional unit of the nervous system.
- Each neuron has three main parts: dendrites, cyton/soma/cell body and axon.
- Dendrites receive impulses from other neurons.
- Cyton/soma processes the impulse.
- Axon transmits the impulse, either to another neuron or to muscles/glands, etc.
- Axon may be myelinated or non-myelinated.
- The impulse transmission is faster in myelinated neurons.
Central Nervous System
The central nervous system (CNS) is made up of the brain and the spinal cord. The functions of different parts of the brain are:
- The cerebrum is responsible for reasoning, logic, emotions, speech, memory, visual processing, recognition of auditory and taste stimuli, etc.
- Cerebellum regulates and coordinates body movements, posture and balance.
- Pons relays signals from…

sections.title: 5. Solved Examples
sections.body:
**Example snippets:**

- Many legumes have Rhizobium nitrogen-fixing bacteria in nodules of their roots, which fix nitrogen from the air for the plant to use; in return, the plants supply sugars to the bacteria.[88] Nitrogen fixed in this way can become available to other plants, and is important in agriculture; for example, farmers may grow a crop rotation of a legume such as beans, followed by a cereal such as wheat, to provide cash crops with a reduced input of nitrogen fertilizer.[89]
- Plants, including trees, appear in mythology, religion, and literature.[128][129][130] In multiple Indo-European, Siberian, and Native American religions, the world tree motif is depicted as a colossal tree growing on the earth, supporting the heavens, and with its roots reaching into the underworld. It may also appear as a cosmic tree or an eagle and serpent tree.[131][132] Forms of the world tree include the archetypal tree of life, which is in turn connected to the Eurasian concept of the sacred tree.[133] Another widespread ancient motif, found for example in Iran, has a tree of life flanked by a pair of confronted animals.[134]

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Control and Coordination**._

**Practice questions from crawled sources:**

1. 1. 60% of the human brain is composed of fat.
2. 2. The brain contains about 100 billion neurons and 100 trillion connections.
3. 3. The texture of the brain is similar to that of firm jelly.

**Additional syllabus-based set** _(auto-generated for review — Phase-II ingest)_

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Nervous system** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Control and Coordination.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Nervous system** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Reflex action** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Control and Coordination.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Reflex action** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Plant hormones** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Control and Coordination.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Plant hormones** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.

sections.title: 11. Video Transcript Summary
sections.body:
_Teacher recap — what a strong Class 10 video lesson on **Control and Coordination** should cover:_

**Lesson flow (as if narrated on screen):**

1. **Hook** — Start with a daily-life example tied to Control and Coordination, not a textbook definition.
2. **Nervous system** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
3. **Reflex action** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
4. **Plant hormones** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
5. **Endocrine system** — Show a labelled diagram or quick demo, then state the CBSE definition in one sentence.
6. **Common mistake** — Warn about the error students make most often in exams.
7. **Recap** — Three bullet points to remember before moving to practice questions.

**After watching, you should be able to:**

- Explain Nervous system in everyday language.
- Draw or describe one diagram related to Control and Coordination.
- Attempt at least two short-answer questions without notes.

**Videos linked for this chapter:**

- 🔍 [CBSE class 10 Control and Coordination NCERT explained Hindi English](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=CBSE+class+10+Control+and+Coordination+NCERT+explained+Hindi+English)
- 🔍 [Control and Coordination class 10 one shot revision](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=Control+and+Coordination+class+10+one+shot+revision)
- 🔍 [NCERT Science class 10 Control and Coordination animation](https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=NCERT+Science+class+10+Control+and+Coordination+animation)
